name: Apple Pie in Cheddar Crust
recipeIngredient: 7 cups Apples (use tart varieties like McIntosh or Granny Smith)
recipeInstructions:
1. Core, peel, and thinly slice the apples.
2. Mix the apples with sugar, flour, cinnamon, butter (or margarine), egg yolk, and water in a bowl.
3. Preheat the oven to 400°F (200°C).
4. Combine the flour, cheese, and salt in a bowl. Using a pastry blender or two knives, cut in the shortening until coarse crumbs form.
5. Add the iced water (about 2-3 cups) and toss with a fork until a dough forms.
6. Press the dough into a ball and divide it almost in half.
7. Roll out the larger half on a lightly floured surface to a 13-inch circle and line a 9-inch pie plate with it, trimming the edges 1/2-inch beyond the rim.
8. Combine the apples, sugar, flour, cinnamon, butter (or margarine), egg yolk, and water in another bowl, mixing well.
9. Arrange the apple mixture in the pastry-lined pie plate.
10. Roll out the remaining dough to an 11-inch circle. Fold into quarters, cut steam slits in the folds, unfold, and place on top of the filling.
11. Trim the crust 1 inch beyond the rim of the pie plate, fold the top crust under the lower one, and flute to form a ridge around the edge.
12. Brush the top with beaten egg yolk mixture.
13. Bake for 45-50 minutes or until apples are tender and the crust is golden brown. Cool on a wire rack until slightly warm before serving with vanilla ice cream, if desired.
